def: v.n. To be petrified; to become hard, to indurate, &c. ( = patthar honā, q.v.s.v. patthar).
more: v. n. 1. To be pe/?/d. 2. To become hard (a boil); to indurate. Rāh dekhte dekhte āṅkheṅ pathrā gaīṅ. pathrāo . Stoning; the act of stoning. patthrā chauth , n. f. The 4th day of the 2nd fortnight in Bhādon. It is considered a bad omen to see the moon on this day if one has not seen her on the second day of this fortnight, and, to cast off its influence, the unlucky person throws stones into his neighbour's house. The abuse he receives in return wards off the evil effects. Hence the name pathra-chauth. patthar barasnā , v. n. To hail. patthar paṛnā , v. n. 1. To fall (stones); to be crushed under stones, or hailstones. Jā, jal-gaë, tujh pe patthar paṛeṅ! Hin. Wom. Away! fire burn you! may stones fall on you. 2. To be damaged, ruined; destroyed. 3. (Ironic.) To make a grand display ( āg lagānā , (5), used by Women. patthar-phoṛ , n. m. One who breaks stones on the road. patthar pheknā , v…
v. n. 1. To be petrified. 2. To become hard (a bile), to indurate. آنکهـ پتهراني See under آنکهـ.

To be fixed or immovable; to be still as a statue. 4. To congeal; freeze. 5. To be stone-blind. 6. To become useless or unserviceable.

Meri sās hai sāṭh baras kī, Apne man se rahat paṭhyā! Wom. Rus. song. My mother-in-law is in her sixtieth year, In her own mind she's still a lusty steer!

more: n. f. S. प्रवृत्ति, , Pr. pauttī news, S. पत्री a letter. 1. A letter; note ( chiṭṭhī ); message. Chīr phāṛ patyā karūṅ, uṅglī qalam dawāt, Nain poṅchh siyāhī karūṅ, likh detī do bāt . Dohā. For paper I will tear my wrap, my finger be my pen, My eyes' lamp-black shall serve for ink, two lines my love I'll pen. 2. Kanak-patr kāgad bhayo, mas bhayo mānak mol, Kaun gunāh ham ne kīyo, likh na sakī do bol? (From the husband to his two wives). Is paper grown as dear as gold, or ink as jewels sold, What's my offence that not a word to me you've writ or told. Kar kampe, likhnī ṭare, aṅg aṅg tharrāë, Sudh āve, chhāti phaṭe, pātī likhī na jāë . The 1st wife's reply. My hand shakes so, my pen doth slip, my body quakes all o'er, Your image thinking on my full heart bursts—I cant write more. Pātī to wā ko likhūṅ jo koī hoë bides, Tan meṅ, man meṅ bas rahyo, tāko kahā saṅdes? The 2nd wife's reply. A l…

def: s.m. A plank; a plank with low side supports (to sit on); a plank on which a washerman beats clothes; a long thick plank used as a harrow; a board on which flour is kneaded, &c.; — unripe grain, Cicer arietinum
subs: (-par), To pass the paṭṛā (over land), to harrow
v.t. To make level, to level, raze, demolish, destroy, lay waste; to deprive of power or strength, render helpless or powerless; to convict (an adversary) and leave him without reply.

s.m. = S پترانك पत्रांक patrāṅk, s.m. See s.v. patra, patr.
n. f. The queen of principal wife of a Rājā. Jamnā paṭ-rānī .
(پاٿ A throne, and راني A queen) s. f. The queen or principal wife of a Rājā.
(پاٿ A throne, and راني A queen) s. f. A queen who is installed or consecrated with the king.
s.m. = S پترانگ पत्रांग patrāṅg, s.m. See s.v. patra, patr.
s.f. (masc. pātratwa), The condition of being a receptacle or a recipient; capacity, fitness, propriety, worthiness, dignity.
adj. & s.m. Drinking; drinker; — protecting, nourishing; protector, &c.
s.f. Letter, note, epistle (see patra).
more: n. f. A daughter ( beṭī ). pālak puttr, pus-put , n. m. An adopted son.
n. f. 1. A narrow slip of wood or metal. 2. The banks of a canal; a raised roadway for foot-passengers. 3. An avenue. 3. A bangle of brass, silver, or gold.

def: adj. & s.m. Winged, feathered; — a bird; an arrow; a person riding on any vehicle; a mountain.

s.f. A narrow slip of wood or metal; a board, slate or slab (for writing on; syn. paṭiyā); the vanes of a Venetian window; a tile; the banks of a canal, &c.; an avenue; a firm seat on horseback; an armlet or bracelet of brass, gilt, gold, or silver.
more: s. f. 1. A plank to write on. 2. A narrow slip or plank of wood, iron, &c. 3. The vanes of a Venetian window. 4. A firm seat on horseback. 5. A title.
s. f. (dakh.) A gilt brass ring, a quarter of an inch broad, worn by women on the wrist ( Qanoon-e-islam ).

Vessel (in general), goblet, bowl, cup, plate, dish, jar, pot; utensil; receptacle, recipient, repository, proper object; what holds or supports; king's counsellor or minister; the bed of a river; a leaf; — capable, worthy, fit, eligible, deserving, able; accomplished (used in comp.).
s.m. A kind of bird, the fly-catcher, Merops viridis; the fork-tailed shrike, or king-crow (syn. bhujang).
more: n. m. The common drongo or king crow; the small black bird with a long forked tail.
s. m. Name of a bird. (Merops viridis).

ety: S. पत्त्त्र+कः; Prk. पत्तं, or पत्तओ
def: s.m. Leaf (of a tree); a card; an ornament worn in the upper part of the ear; a leaf-shaped ornament hanging from an ear-ring
more: n. m. 1. A leaf (of a tree). 2. A card. 1. Wohī ḍhāk ke tīn pāt . Prov. The Dhāk stem has but three leaves. (Do what I will I can never get any more—usually said by domestics and subordinate officers whose pay is never raised). 2. Koī hukm bin uske chaltā nahiṅ, Kahīṅ ek pattā bhī hiltā nahīṅ . Sohan Lal. Without his leave nothing can move, No leaf can stir of tree or grove. 3. A leaf-shaped ornament hanging from an ear-ring. Bālī, patte, jhūmke! Street cry. pattā toṛ bhāgnā . v. n. To run as fast as one's legs will carry him; take to one's heels. (So called from a boy's game called ātī pātī , q. v. in which the runner is required to pluck a leaf from a certain tree and catch one of the rest who have scampered off). Rupayā lete hī pattā-toṛ bhāg gayā .

n. m. S. पत्र leaf, पट्ट a board. 1. A board on which Hindus sit while eating their meals or performing religious ceremonies (More com. paṭrā ). 2. A fuller's board. 3. A dog or horse collar. 4. The gold or silver threads of an old native shoe. 5. A shoulder strap and badge. 6. A deed, commonly a title deed to land, or a lease. 7. A code or book of regulations for the guidance of rent-collectors. 8. Unripe grain (Cicer arietinum) . E. Āi tohar beṭā bahut jiyān kaëlak, paṭṭā chhīmī toṛī lāel . Tir. Your son has done great damage today, he has gone and plucked a lot of unripe peas. paṭṭā utārnā , v. a. To discharge from service. paṭṭā-pher , n. m. The conclusion of a Hindu marriage ceremony in which the bride and bridegroom change seats. paṭṭā tuṛānā , v. n. To get loose; to run away. Kuttā paṭṭā tuṛā gayā. paṭṭā ṭheke-dārī , n. A farming lease.
s. m. 1. A leaf. 2. A trinket. پتاهونا pattā honā, To run away, to flee.
subs: v.n. To run as fast as one can, to take to one's heels (so called from a boy's game in which the runner has to pluck a leaf from a certain tree and catch one of the rest who have run off)
v.n. To run off, run away, flee.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit पत्तिअ (pattia), from Sanskrit प्रत्याययति (pratyāyayati, “convinces”).

def: s.m. A board on which Hindūs sit whilst eating their meals or performing religious ceremonies; a fuller's board, board, table; seat; a lock of hair, ringlet, curl; the collar of a horse, or of a dog, &c.; girth of a horse; shoulder-strap and badge (of a peon, &c.); gold or silver threads of an old native shoe; an ornament (worn by women as an appendage to the bālī); an ornament for the forehead, frontlet; a deed (com. a title-deed to land), a deed of lease; a code or book of regulations for the guidance of rent-collectors
more: s. m. 1. A lock of hair. 2. A dogcollar. 3. Name of harness.
s. m. A deed, particularly a title deed to land or a deed of lease. پٿّاتڙانا paṭṭā tuṛānā, To flee, run away.
s. m. (dakh.) A stock (band for the neck). ¶ puṭṭā, adj. Fatherless ( Harris ).
s. m. (dakh.) Name of an ornament for the neck worn by women ( Qanoon-e-islam ).
subs: (-kā), To take off the strap and badge, to dismiss from service
s.m. Exchange of seats by the bride and bridegroom at the conclusion of a (Hindu) marriage ceremony
v.n. 'To break the collar and go off'; to get loose, to run away
paṭṭā ṭheke-dārī, s.m. A farming lease
s.m. Lease granted to tenants individually
s.m. Granter of a lease, lessor
s.m. Fine, fee, or complimentary present of money, on the grant of a lease
adj. & adv. According to shares or assessment.

def: Ped. for H. girvī paṭṭa . A mortgage lease.
A fine, fee, or complimentary present of money on the grant of a lease. paṭṭā qubūlīyat , n. m. The settlement paper.

s.m. Slab, tablet (for painting); plate (of metal, for inscription or engraving of royal edicts, grants, &c.); patent, document, royal grant or order (written on copper, stone, &c); seat, chair, throne; a strip; frontlet; turban, tiara, diadem; woven silk; an upper or outer garment; bandage, ligature, &c.; city, town, village; — the plant Corchorus olitorius (from the fibres of the bark of which, called jute, a coarse sackcloth and cordage are prepared)
subs: paṭṭaj, s.m. A kind of canvas or sackcloth
paṭṭ-rānī, s.f. The principal queen, queen-consort ( = paṭ-rānī; pāt-rānī)
s.m. The plant Cœsalpina sappan (used in dyeing)

def: Ped. for H. paṭait . A lease holder; lessee. paṭṭā dene-wālā . The grantor of a lease; lessor.
A private lease.
s.m. See s.v. paṭṭa. s.m. Leaf (of a tree); a card; an ornament worn in the upper part of the ear; a leaf-shaped ornament hanging from an ear-ring

ety: irr. Ar. pl. of paṭṭa = paṭṭā
def: s.m. Deeds of lease, title-deeds to land.
s.m. Document; bandage; frontlet (see paṭṭa and paṭṭā).
s.f. A plate or trencher made of leaves; a plate of cakes, sweetmeats, &c.
more: n. f. S. पत्रावली 1. Leaves sewn together and serving as a plate to eat out of. 2. A plate of cakes, laḍḍū , and other sweetmeats, etc. 1. Byāh pīchhe pattal bhārī . Prov. The marriage feast done with and o'er, a crust of bread to give is dolour sore. 2. Khāyā, pattal phāṛ ḍālī. pattal bāṅdh'nā , v. a. To bar the feast against the guests. It consists in barring the guests from beginning their meal, till one of their number shall have given the answer to a riddle which is proposed by one of the women. The answer is called pattal kholnā , i. e. permitting the guests to sit to the meal. 1. Ujhak jharokan sundar nārī, dehī sakal sajnan ko gārī, Bāṅdhī pattal bhog lagāve, mūrakh panchan māṅh kahāve . Wom. The fair ones sprang and from the windows showered abuse on all, 'The meal is barred—a fool is he who on the food will fall.' 2. Sirī gur-deo Basisṭ soṅ kahī Dasrath yeh bāt, 'Pattal kholo p…
s. f. A plate or trencher formed of leaves.
subs: v.n. To withhold or bar the meal or repast (this is done till one of the guests shall have solved the riddle which is proposed by one of the women of the party)
v.n. To open the repast, commence the meal (on the solution of the proposed riddle).

def: s.m. = pātāl, q.v. s.m. One of the seven regions under the earth and the abode of the Nāgas or serpents and demons; hell, the infernal regions; — hole, chasm, excavation; submarine fire; — a sort of apparatus for calcining and subliming metals (formed of two earthen pots, the upper one inverted over the lower, and the two joined together by their necks with cement and placed in a hole containing fire)
more: n. m. Infernal regions. See پاتال Pattāl kī khabar lātā hai! (He is so very sharp!).
s. m. The infernal regions or a place under the earth.
1. s. See پتال pattāl. 2. adj. (dakh.) Deep.
subs: s.f. The Ganges of the infernal regions.

s.m. = patthar, q.v. s.m. Stone, rock; precious stone, gem; hailstone; anything hard, troublesome, trying, intolerable, &c.; — adj. Hard; hard-hearted; heavy; dull; difficult; disagreeable, intolerable, &c.
more: n. m. S. पितृ Ancestors; manes of the dead. pitr-ārpan . Gifts in honor of deceased relatives, distributed at the Sharādhs or funeral ceremonies. pitr-asthān , n. m. The place where the manes of one's ancestors are deposited. pitr-bandhū , n. m. A cognate relation in the paternal line. pitr-paksh , n. f. 1. The first or dark fortnight of Āsauj when the Hindūs celebrate the customary obsequies to the manes of their ancestors ( kanāgat ). pittar pānī ānā , v. n. Hin. Wom. To be appeased, satisfied. pitr-tith , n. f. The last day of the first fortnight in Āsauj . pitr-tarpan , n. m. An offering of water to the dead. pitr-tīrath , n. m. 1. Gayā, or the city so called, where the performance of funeral rites is held to be peculiarly efficacious. 2. The part between the fore-finger and thumb, sacred to the manes. pitr-dān, pitr-ārpan , n. m. Gifts in honor of one's deceased ancestors. pitr-shar…

n. m. 1. A leaf (of a tree or book). 2. A plate of metal, chiefly of copper; an alloy of silver and copper. 3. A letter; document; deed. 4. A grant or conveyance of land engraven on a plate of metal. udhār-patr , n. m. A note of hand; a promissory note ( tamassuk ). bintī-patr , n. m. A petition ( aṛzī ); memorial. maut-patr , n. def: 1. s. m. Name of a sort of greens. 2. A skinflint. 3. Name of a serpent. 4. Name of a fish. 5. adj. One attached to his own house, who remains there even in distress.
s. (dakh.) Lichen rotundatus, Lin. or rock lichen ( Qanoon-e-islam, gloss. p. lxiv).
s.m. Stone, rock; precious stone, gem; hailstone; anything hard, troublesome, trying, intolerable, &c.; — adj. Hard; hard-hearted; heavy; dull; difficult; disagreeable, intolerable, &c.
more: n. m. S. पत्थर 1. A stone; rockstone. 2. A millstone. 1. Patthar ko joṅk nahīṅ lagtī , Prov. No leech sticks to a stone. 2. Mom ho to pigle, kahīṅ patthar bhī piglā hai? Prov. Wax would melt, but could you ever melt a stone? (Said of the miserly and hard-hearted). 3. A precious stone; jewel; gem; diamond. 4. A hailstone. 5. Anything hard, troublesome, disgusting, intolerable, etc. patthar , adj. 1. Hard; inflexible; admantine; hard as iron. 2. Hard-hearted; cruel; pitiless; inexorable. 3. Heavy; burdensome; immoveable; intolerable. Merī chhātī par do patthar baiṭhe haiṅ . Two heavy stones lie on my breast. (Two daughters to marry). 4. Dull; idle; lazy. 5. Stupid; foolish. 6. Hard; difficult; puzzling; intricate. 7. Hateful; odious; abominable; disgusting.

s. m. A stone, stone. پتهربازي patthar-bāzī, s. f. Stone-throwing, playing with stones. پتهرپاني هوجانا patthar pānī ho jānā, 1. The melting of a stony heart. 2. A difficult task to be facilitated. پتهرپسيجنا patthar pasījnā, To be softened or melted (a stony heart). پتهرچهاتي پررکهنا patthar chhātī par rakhnā, To be patient by compulsion or from being without remedy. پتهرساپهينک مارنا patthar sā phenk mārnā, To reply rashly without understanding the meaning of oneʼs question. پتهرسي سرپهوڙنايامارنا patthar se sir phoṛnā or mārnā, To teach or instruct a fool. پتهرهونا patthar honā, 1. To be heavy. 2. To be stable, to stand still. 3. To be unmerciful. (پتهري patthare occurs in Mīr Taḳī as a plural; so لگواٴي پتهري).

wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀧𑀢𑁆𑀣𑀭 m (patthara, “stone”) without cluster simplification, from Sanskrit प्रस्तर (prastara). Displaced Old Hindi पाथर (pāthara). Cognate with Punjabi پَتَّھر (pattahr). False cognate of Ancient Greek πέτρα (pétra).

def: s. m. Name of a plant. (Plectranthus aromaticus, Roxb. ).
s. m. A woodpecker. (Picus).
E. n. m. The locks of hair on either side.
s.m. A kind of stone weight.
s.f. Grit, gravel, stone; a flint; hone, whetstone; stone or gravel in the bladder, calculus; a gizzard.
more: n. f. 1. Gravel; stone. 2. Flint. 3. A hone. 4. Stone in the bladder; calculus. 5. A gizzard.
s. f. 1. Grit, gravel. 2. The flint of a musket. 3. Stone in the bladder, calculus. 4. A gizzard.

def: s.m. Pedestrian; foot-soldier; — s.f. The smallest division of an army, company, platoon (consisting of one chariot, one elephant, three horsemen, and five foot-soldiers)
s.f. Leaf; leaves; hemp-leaves (of which an intoxicating potion is made); a leaf or thin plate (of metal); a hem; a share ( = paṭṭī)
more: n. f. 1. Leaf or leaves; leaflet. 2. A share ( hissah ). 3. A thin plate of metal. pattī-dār , n. m. A shareholder.

n. f. 1. A part; portion; a division of a village; a division of land into separate portions or strips. 2. ( In law .) An original or separate share in a joint or coparcenary village or estate comprising many villages. 3. A strip of cloth; a bandage; plaster; fillet. 4. A narrow slip of paper or metal. 5. A head-band or narrow plate of silver or brass worn across the turban. 6. A dashing blade; a gay young spark. 7. A rafter, or the whole row or series of rafters that cover one side of a house. 8. A side of a bedstead. 1. Paṭṭi se paṭṭī milā-ke khāṭ bichhātī hai! Wom. Their beds are joined together side by side! (Such a loving couple as they are! Or, she is so closely watched). 2. Maiṅ kyā teṛī paṭṭī tale kī hūṅ . Wom. Prov. Am I in aught your inferior? 9. (S. पट् to go) A long straight run. Ghoṛe ko paṭṭī do . 10. Parting of the hair in the middle or side. 1. Kahīṅ apnī paṭṭī saṅvāre ko…

A row. 5. A kind of sweetmeat. 6. Division of the hair which is combed towards the two sides and divided by a line in the middle. 7. ( पट्ट or पट्टी ) A written order or patent.

n. f. S. पवन from पू to make clean. 1. Air; wind; ether; atmosphere. Paun meṅ paun mile, pānī meṅ pānī . Prov. Air with air mingling, water with water. (Dust to dust, ashes to ashes). 2. The breath of life; the spirit. Nau duāre kā pīṅjrā, jis meṅ panchhī, paun. Rahne kā hai āchraj, gaë achambā kaun? The Soul Kabīr. Nine doors, one cage, for birds within but air, The wonder is 'tis there, not flown elsewhere. 3. A spell; charm; magic. Sīyāne ne paun mārī. paun pānī kā asar . s.m. = pau-sālā, q.v.s.v. pau. s.f. See s.v. pau. s.f. Place for watering cattle; a shed on the road-side for accommodating travellers with water, place where water is distributed, a drinking fount ( = pauh, q.v.)
s.m. = pūs, q.v. s.m. The ninth solar month of the Hindūs (Dec.-Jan., the full moon of which is near pushya, three stars in Cancer).
s.f. The night or day of full moon in the month paush or pūs (Dec.-Jan.).
s.f. A lotus-pool; a large pond, reservoir.
s.m. The twenty-eighth nakshatra or lunar mansion.
adj. Relating to or belonging to flowers; made of flowers; flowery, floral.
s.m. Oxide of brass (used as a collyrium), green vitriol.
adv. Relating to or promoting growth or welfare; nutritious, nourishing, nutritive, fattening, invigorating; preservative, protective.
s. (dakh.) A kind of lamp ( Qanoone-islam ).
s.f. A kind of lamp.

s.m. A grandson ( = potā, q.v.). s.m. The young of any animal; a young elephant ten years old; a young plant or tree; the young shoot of a plant; a fetus which has no enveloping membrane; cloth, a garment; the site or foundation of a house; a vessel, ship, boat
subs: potaj, s.m. An animal which is produced or developed from a fetus which has no enveloping membrane (as an elephant and certain other quadrupeds, which are supposed, in the physiology of the Hindūs, to carry their young without the aid of an ovarium or uterus).

def: s.f. A son's daughter, granddaughter.
adj. Of or belonging to a son or to a grandson.
s.m. An image-worshipper, idolater.
s.m. = پوا pawwā, q.v. s.m. A weight or measure of a quarter of a ser; a quarter; a quart measure.
s.f. Chain for the leg, fetter, stocks; — the leg of a boot or stocking; an odd shoe or slipper.
more: n. f. S. पाद foot. 1. One foot of a boot or stocking. 2. An odd shoe or slipper.

def: s. f. 1. A chain fastened to horsesʼ legs to prevent their being stolen. 2. Chains with which the legs of a criminal are fastened, fetters. 3. The leg of a boot or stocking.
s.m. Mean people. &c. (see pājī).

adj. & s.m. Cleansing, purifying; purificatory; pertaining to Agni; pure; — Agni, the deified personification of fire; fire (in general); a saint or person purified by religious abstraction; — a species of tree, Premna integrifolia or spinosa (the wood of which is used to procure fire by attrition); a species of plant, leadwort, Plumbago zeylanica; the plant Semecarpus anacardium, a plant used as a vermifuge, Carthamus tinctorius.
adj. Purifying, purificatory.
s.m. Wind, air; — name of a particular fire (regarded as a son of Agni); — an epithet of the moon.

adj. & s.m. Purifying, purificatory, purgatory, freeing from sin, sanctifying; purified, pure, holy; — purifier; fire (as purifying or cleansing); water; cowdung; incense; austere devotion, penance, expiation; — the seed of the plant Elœocarpus ganitus (of which rosaries are made); a species of grass, Costus speciosus
s. m. plur. (from पाद ) Feet.

s.m.f. The act of purifying, purification; winnowing; 'the purifier,' wind, air; the Regent of the winds and of the north-west quarter; the wind of the body; the breath of life; — a householder's sacred fire; — spell, charm; the evil spirits appointed by a wizard or magician to injure any one
subs: s.m. Taking the air, walking or riding for diversion or exercise ( = hawā khānā)
or paun-pānī, s.m. 'Wind and water,' climate ( = āb o hawā)
s.f. Ascertaining the direction of the wind
or paun-sūt, s.m. = pavan-kā pūt, s.m. The son of Rāvaṇ, the ape Hanumān
v.t. To enchant; to injure by the aid of malignant spirits
s.m. An arrow projected by means of air; an arrow which when projected raises the wind
s.m. Morbid condition of wind; rheumatism, &c.

def: s.m. A species of grain, Andropogon saccharatus ( = yavanāl).
s.m. = paṅwāṛā q.v.
more: s. (dakh.) i. q. قصي کابيان ḳiṣṣe kā bayān, Explanation of a story.
